about.historyLink: 📜 Read the full history of the Saint and the Temple →
about.heritageDesc: More than 200 years ago, Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu, a realized spiritual luminary, attained Jeeva Samadhi at this holy site.
about.desc: The Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu Trust has been established to systematically manage, maintain, and develop the sacred Jeeva Samadhi (Brindavan) at Parnapalli.
about.title: A Sacred Legacy of Two Centuries
about.visionDesc: To develop Sri Omkareswara Swamy Temple into a vibrant spiritual, cultural, and charitable centre serving devotees and seekers at large.

vision.p1: In 2026, we resolve to expand facilities including a Yagnashala, priest quarters, and visitor rooms. We pray to the Great Saint to guide us in fulfilling these sacred objectives.
vision.p2: On January 31, 2026 (Vishwasu Nama Samvatsara Magha Shuddha Trayodashi), we officially formed the Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu Trust (Reg. No. 13/2026) for Sanatana Dharma protection.

vision.shloka: “यत्र समाधिस्थो महायोगी, तत्र शिवानुग्रहः नित्यः प्रवहति ॥”
vision.meaning: Where the great yogi abides in Jeeva Samadhi, there the grace of Lord Shiva flows eternally.

gurukrpa.p1: Smt. Chayamma passed away on April 9, 1951 (Khara Nama Samvatsara Chaitra Shuddha Tadiya) at Tadimarri village. Following her departure, Sri VenkataRamayya increasingly immersed himself in spiritual contemplation.
gurukrpa.p2: During this time, he received the grace of the Kanchi Paramacharya, Sri Chandrasekharendra Saraswati (Periyava), regarded as a living embodiment of Adi Shankara. He received the Acharya's Padukas and cloth as sacred Prasada. When the Paramacharya suggested Sannyasa, he humbly declined due to his responsibility toward his youngest son's marriage.
gurukrpa.p3: Though four of his sons moved to Hyderabad, he remained dedicated to Sanatana Dharma practices in Parnapalli. To serve him and his sisters, his fourth son stayed back as a teacher, ensuring their traditional way of life remained undisturbed until their final moments.
gurukrpa.p4: While unwell during his final days, he continuously chanted "Sri Rama". On the auspicious day of November 5, 1973 (Pramadeecha Nama Samvatsara Karthika Shuddha Ekadashi), he surrendered himself at the lotus feet of Sri Ramachandra at the age of 78.

jivanmukta.p1: The lineage traces back to Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu. Since he had no children, he adopted Chinna Narasayya (his brother Sri Ashwatthamayya's son). Although a householder, he attained deep Vairagya and became a Jivanmukta.
jivanmukta.p2: VenkataRama Bhotlu entered Sajiva Samadhi on the banks of the Chitravati River on Margashirsha Shuddha Vidiya. The exact age is unknown, but a family tree written by VenkataRamayya's father confirms this as the 6th generation.
jivanmukta.p3: Since then, the tradition of Vedic study and Sanatana Dharma practice has been maintained in every generation of the Valiveru lineage.

temple.p1: Remarkably, Sri VenkataRamayya's fourth son, Sri Lakshmi Narasayya, was born on the same Margashirsha Shuddha Vidiya as the Saint’s Samadhi day. A raised platform had been built at the Samadhi site where farmers once rested.
temple.p2: Sri Lakshmi Narasayya contributed funds to renovate the Samadhi platform. Now, the 6th generation has constructed a new temple and consecrated a Narmada Banalingam as Sri Omkareshwara Swamy on November 3, 2019.
temple.p3: Brahmotsavams were held grandly until 2024. In 2025, following the instructions of Kanchi Bala Periyava (Sri Vijayendra Saraswati), the Aradhana began to be conducted strictly according to scriptural tradition.
temple.p4: Situated on the Anantapur-Kadapa highway, the shrine has gained great prominence, with many devotees visiting to find their heartfelt prayers answered through the Saint's miracles.

invocation.v1: oṃ śrī gurubhyo namaḥ | oṃ śrī mahāgaṇādhipataye namaḥ | oṃ śrī sarasvatyai namaḥ | oṃ śrī sadguru sāyināthāya namaḥ ||

īśvaro gururātmeti mūrtibheda vibhāgine |
vyomavatvyāpta dehāya śrī dakṣiṇāmūrtaye namaḥ ||

s1.name: Smt. Sheshamma
s1.desc: Widowed young, she spent her life in her brother's home immersed in the remembrance of Sri Rama, attaining Shivaikya on October 23, 1973, one week before her brother.

s2.name: Smt. Kamakshamma
s2.desc: Married Sri Modali Vishwanatha Sharma of Anantapur; mother of Sanskrit scholar Sri Bala Vishwanatha Sharma of Sri Sathya Sai College.

s3.name: Sri Narasayya
s3.desc: He was VenkataRamayya's younger brother. He was a teacher in Dharmavaram and eventually settled in Bengaluru, leading a life of Sanatana Dharma until his passing on October 18, 1986.

roots.p1: Parnapalli is a village situated on the banks of the Chitravati River in Pulivendula taluk of Kadapa district, Andhra Pradesh. In this village lived the late Sri VenkataRamayya, a Shrotriya, Vedic scholar of the Velanadu tradition belonging to the Gautama Gotra, bearing the family name Valiveru.
roots.p2: The family deity (Gṛha Daiva) of the Valiveru household is Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy of Doddadalavatam (Madhugiri taluk, Karnataka). It is still a tradition to worship Sri Ramachandra Murthy as the cherished Iṣṭa Daiva within the Valiveru family.
roots.p3: Sri VenkataRamayya and Smt. Chayamma were blessed with five sons. He had two sisters—Smt. Sheshamma and Smt. Kamakshamma—and a younger brother Sri Narasayya. Sri Narasayya was later adopted by his uncle Sri Sitaramayya.
roots.image_caption_handwritten: The Handwritten family tree mentioned in the records.
roots.image_caption_typed: The Typed version of the family tree for clarity.

history.title: Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu

footer.address: Omkara Kshetram, Paarnapalli Village, Kadapa District, Andhra Pradesh — 516396

acharana.p1: Sri VenkataRamayya lived a life of simplicity and contentment despite material poverty. He studied the Vedas thoroughly and upheld the principles of Sanatana Dharma with sincerity and discipline through total Vedic conduct.
acharana.p2: His daily life followed a sacred rhythm: Trikala Sandhya, morning Panchayatana Puja, and Abhisheka. During afternoons, he would sit on the front porch and recite the Mahabharata, Bhagavata, and Ramayana, explaining the teachings to the villagers. Hospitality was abundant; many mahatmas traveling through the village find refuge in his home.
acharana.p3: He spent his time in Vedantic contemplation and acted with practical wisdom. It is no exaggeration to say that for him, Vedanta was a lived reality—a fact praised by all who knew him.

hero.meaning1: Three things are rare and due to the grace of God: human birth, longing for liberation, and the refuge of a great soul.
hero.desc1: Centered around the divine Jeeva Samadhi (Brindavan) of the revered saint Brahmasri Valiveru VenkataRama Bhotlu, this sacred site in Parnapalli is graced by the Sri Omkareswara Swamy BanaLinga, established directly upon the saint's eternal presence bestowing blessings upon all devotees and seekers.

footer.aboutDesc: Dedicated to the sacred service and development of Sri Omkareswara Swamy Temple, Parnapalli. The Trust preserves temple traditions, promotes Sanatana Dharma, and serves devotees and seekers.
